1: Naught be all else to me
6: Saints find a refuge in their God,

callout_texts: What a wonderful and demanding parental vocation: calling children Godward in faith and sending them into the world to suffer as right-eous followers of Jesus who take refuge in God.
In many ways, parenting amounts to preparing our children to live faithfully in a dangerous world—a world rife with dark valleys, deadly shadows, evil forces, and hostile enemies.
While parents need spiritual vigilance in order to rightly parent their children in a godless society, they also need childlike faith to entrust themselves to God’s safekeeping.
A godly home fosters a sense of justice in its collective consciousness such that it exerts the influence of its countercultural lifestyle on the surrounding world.
The fear of the Lord establishes a hearty and unfeigned faith in God that outfits our growing children to withstand unspeakable pain and difficulty.
Godly parents aim for children to experience God for themselves in all his glory, his goodness, his righteousness, and his gracious deliverance.
While parents raise their children in the midst of a godless society, they also do battle against he depraved and self-promoting human heart.
Building home is like planting and cultivating a garden where seeds of infants and sprout, grow, blossom, and bear fruit for righteousness.
The personal heritage and blessing that is a godly family is also a redemptive blessing and spiritual influence to our fallen world.
Through the family, society is shaped, the community is formed, the church is established, and the generations build God’s kingdom.
The Christian family is designed by God to incubate a countercultural lifestyle of biblical belief, godly living, and holy justice.
Genuine faith in God that is expressed in a holy fear of God results in deep, dynamic, and enduring change in life and character.
Building hearth and home is set within the larger endeavor of establishing God’s people and securing the best for larger society.
The aim of parenting is to release the next generation into the world, rooted in uncompromising godliness and fervent faith.
A well-ordered home aligned after God’s design is one of our greatest testimonies in and defenses against a godless society.
Building a godly household is one of the main ways we can have influence in God’s covenant people and in surrounding world.
Christian homes that are secure in God’s stronghold can in turn become a safe haven for the needy and helpless around them.
God nurtures and uplifts, feeds and encourages, and nourishes and rejuvenates. He sustains body and fortifies soul.
Homebuilding in piety—encouraging genuine faith in and earnest obedience to God—can have a generational impact.
A child dedicated in each Christian home must ultimately be devoted to the Christ child dedicated in Jerusalem.
But God is more than just our parenting pattern. He is the safety and security to which all godly families run.
For all believers, to be devoted to God is to be dedicated to God’s people. One cannot exist without the other.
Childrearing is not ultimately a matter of deference to parental authority, but of fostering devotion to God.
Done according to God’s design, few vocations like Christian homebuilding make a greater impact in the world.
In order to build Christian hearth and home in a godly way, we must be grounded in godly purpose and mission
The personal faith of the individual members of a household make up the real collective character of a home.
In his revolt against the Lord, the prince of darkness marks the household as one of his most hated targets.
Parents serve their family by cultivating habits of worship early on in the lives of their children.
Godly parenting in the fear of the Lord teaches children to relish the Lord as much as revere him.
Our faithful presence with our children can be one of our greatest ministry modes to our children.
Faith formation in the home and in the faith community aims to nurture people in devotion to God.
We often indirectly “pick up” as much from our family culture as we do from direct instruction.
The redemptive story faithfully recorded in the Bible is the faith heritage of every believer.
The Christian family is the glad beneficiary of the faith from saints in prior generations.
Christian homebuilding is a matter of establishing our homes on the foundation of Christ.
A faithful shepherd, God leads his people securely from this life into the world to come.
Parenting work done in a shepherding mode emulates God himself—the Shepherd of shepherds
Parents are best caretakers of their children by being faithful custodians of the truth.
Holiness and intimacy enjoyed between husband and wife irrigate the garden of the home.
Our very first acts as Christian parents should not be child-centric but God-focused.
God’s compassion for his people is the great pattern and power of all parental love.
Christian hearth-building must be set within the context of the faith community.
It is in the home where children first learn what healthy relationships entail.
At its core, parenting is a work of faith development and moral formation.
Comfort is found in God’s safeguarding rod and his corrective staff.
Parenting work is buoyed by a life of vibrant worship to the Lord.
God’s care for us is our model and means to care for our children.
Relationship with God best outfits us for our parenting vocation.
When God forms a family, he invites us to cultivate a home.
Parents are frontline workers in faith formation.
